caseMetadata.title: The Clock in the Locked Study
caseMetadata.shortDescription: A compact one-room theft mystery for the first end-to-end engine test.

playerConfig.settingSummary: A single locked study in a mid-century private townhouse

setting.place: A private study in a townhouse
setting.socialContext: A formal household with family and staff routines.
setting.technologyLevel: Mid-century domestic technology, mechanical clocks, paper records.
setting.sensoryStyle: Polished wood, clock oil, old paper, lamplight, rain at the window.

centralMystery.openingProblem: A valuable pocket watch vanishes from a locked display case during a small gathering in one study.
centralMystery.stakes: The object is valuable and scheduled for transfer the next morning.
centralMystery.playerObjective: Determine who took the watch, how, why, and where it is now.
centralMystery.successCondition: The player identifies the culprit, explains the prepared case mechanism, establishes motive, and locates the hidden object.

solution.motive: The culprit needed quick money to solve a private financial pressure.
solution.motiveProportionality: The crime is theft, and the object's value is enough to solve the immediate pressure.
solution.method: The culprit prepared a rear service panel on the display case, used a brief distraction to remove the watch without disturbing the front lock, and hid it nearby.
solution.opportunity: A short attention break occurs while the culprit is beside the display case during service duties.
solution.means: Routine access, a small household tool, knowledge of the room, and normal service movement.
solution.concealmentPlan: Use the intact front lock and another suspect's visible money-related issue to misdirect suspicion.
solution.culpritMistake: Trace evidence, a mechanical disturbance, a discoverable motive clue, and an over-specific denial connect the culprit to the theft.

proofRequiredForAccusation.deductionRequired: Connect alternate case access, trace evidence, opportunity, motive, and hiding place.
proofRequiredForAccusation.insufficientAccusationResponse: The accusation needs the hidden object or a firm physical chain.

finalRevealBeats: The front lock was not the access point.
A rear service panel was prepared earlier.
Physical traces connect the preparation to the culprit.
The mechanical clue identifies the hiding place.
A discoverable motive clue explains why the culprit acted.
The red herring has an innocent explanation.

postResolutionAnswers.Why?: A private financial pressure created motive.
postResolutionAnswers.How?: Through the prepared rear service panel rather than the front lock.
postResolutionAnswers.Where was it hidden?: Inside the tallcase clock's lower compartment.

characters.name: Rowan Vale
characters.apparentMotive: Could appear to benefit from dramatizing or insuring the loss.
characters.alibiTruth: He turned away during the opportunity window.
knowledge: The watch was present shortly before the theft.
The display case has a rear service panel.
secrets: He is embarrassed by household money discussions.
liesOrOmissions: Initially downplays the recent request for money.
characters.interrogationGuidance: Cooperative and flustered; useful for context, not for solving the case.

characters.name: Celia Vale
characters.relationshipToMystery: Present during the theft and visibly nervous.
characters.apparentMotive: A private document suggests money trouble.
characters.trueMotiveOrInnocentExplanation: Her private matter is unrelated to the watch.
characters.alibiTruth: She bent to recover a dropped item during the opportunity window.
knowledge: Thomas moved near the case and clock.
Her own private document is unrelated to the watch.
secrets: She is hiding an embarrassing but innocent money matter.
liesOrOmissions: Initially gives a vague explanation for the document.
characters.interrogationGuidance: Defensive at first, then clarifies if pressed fairly.

characters.name: Thomas Greer
characters.relationshipToMystery: Has access to the room, case area, and clock.
characters.trueMotiveOrInnocentExplanation: He has a private financial pressure and is the thief.
characters.alibiClaim: He only served and did not touch the case or clock after preparation.
characters.alibiTruth: He prepared the rear panel, used the distraction window, and hid the watch.
liesOrOmissions: Denies touching the clock after preparation.
characters.interrogationGuidance: Polite and precise; becomes strained when evidence is connected.

locations.name: Locked study
locations.description: A study with desk, fireplace, display case, tallcase clock, tea trolley, lamp, and window.

timeline.summary: The culprit receives private financial pressure.
witnessKnowledgeCreated: Rowan knows of a recent request for money.

timeline.summary: The rear service panel is loosened.
timeline.cause: The culprit needs an alternate access point.
timeline.effect: The front lock can remain intact.

timeline.summary: The watch is present while all three are in the room.
timeline.effect: All suspects know it is present.
witnessKnowledgeCreated: The watch was present shortly before the theft.

timeline.summary: A brief attention break occurs while the culprit is beside the case.
timeline.cause: Two witnesses are momentarily looking elsewhere.
timeline.effect: The culprit has a few seconds of opportunity.
witnessKnowledgeCreated: Celia recalls Thomas beside the case.

timeline.summary: The watch is removed through the prepared panel.
timeline.cause: The panel and opportunity window align.
timeline.effect: The object leaves the case while the front lock remains intact.

timeline.summary: The object is hidden in the clock, disturbing the mechanism.
timeline.cause: The culprit needs a temporary hiding place.
timeline.effect: The clock becomes an evidence source.

clues.title: Loose rear service panel
clues.playerFacingDescription: The front lock is intact, but a rear service panel is loose.
clues.trueMeaning: The object was removed through the rear panel.
clues.resolvedInFinalReveal: The front lock was not the access point.
clues.textFallback: The rear service panel can lift slightly.

clues.title: Fresh brass dust
clues.playerFacingDescription: Bright brass dust sits below the rear panel screws.
clues.discoveryConditions: Inspect the area below the case.

clues.title: Black thread
clues.playerFacingDescription: A black thread is caught on the case panel.
clues.trueMeaning: It connects the case tampering to the culprit's clothing.
clues.discoveryConditions: Inspect the panel or compare clothing.
clues.resolvedInFinalReveal: The trace links the culprit to the panel.
clues.textFallback: The thread matches the culprit's clothing.

clues.title: Clock oil trace
clues.playerFacingDescription: A disturbed screw carries a faint oil scent.
clues.trueMeaning: The panel handler also handled the clock oil.
clues.discoveryConditions: Inspect the screw and compare with clock oil.
clues.resolvedInFinalReveal: The oil links case tampering and clock access.
clues.textFallback: The same oil smell appears near the clock.

clues.title: Stopped clock
clues.playerFacingDescription: The clock has stopped during the theft window.
clues.trueMeaning: The hidden object disturbed the mechanism.
clues.sourceOrProvenance: The hiding place created the disturbance.
clues.resolvedInFinalReveal: The stopped clock identifies the hiding area.
clues.textFallback: The clock stopped during the theft window.

clues.title: Scraped clock latch
clues.playerFacingDescription: The lower clock latch has a fresh scrape.
clues.sourceOrProvenance: Created when the culprit hid the object.

clues.title: Hidden ticking
clues.playerFacingDescription: A faint second ticking can be heard inside the stopped clock.
clues.trueMeaning: The missing object is hidden inside the clock.
clues.discoveryConditions: Listen to or open the clock compartment.
clues.sourceOrProvenance: The culprit hid the object there.
clues.resolvedInFinalReveal: The hidden object proves the solution.
clues.textFallback: The missing object is inside the clock.

clues.title: Over-specific denial
clues.playerFacingDescription: When asked about his movements, Thomas volunteers a denial about the clock before the player raises it.
clues.trueMeaning: He is too aware of the clock issue.
clues.discoveryConditions: Question Thomas about movements without first raising the clock.
clues.textFallback: Thomas answers more specifically than the question requires.

clues.title: Private money note
clues.playerFacingDescription: A folded note refers to an urgent payment.
clues.trueMeaning: Thomas has a motive for theft.
clues.discoveryConditions: Search near the service area or ask about recent money requests.
clues.textFallback: The note shows urgent financial pressure.

clues.title: Private envelope
clues.playerFacingDescription: A private envelope is visible near Celia's belongings.
clues.trueMeaning: It concerns Celia's own matter and is unrelated to the watch.
clues.discoveryConditions: Notice Celia's nervousness or ask about the envelope.
clues.textFallback: The envelope concerns a different item.

redHerrings.title: Private envelope
redHerrings.innocentExplanation: The matter is unrelated to the stolen watch.
redHerrings.fairnessNotes: Clearable and limited to one red herring.

evidence.title: Display case
evidence.description: A case with an intact front lock and loosened rear panel.
evidence.discoveryConditions: Visible from start; details require inspection.

evidence.title: Black thread
evidence.description: A black fiber caught on the case panel.

evidence.title: Clock oil cloth
evidence.description: Oil cloth used for clock maintenance.

evidence.title: Tallcase clock
evidence.discoveryConditions: Visible from start; clues require inspection or listening.

evidence.title: Private money note
evidence.description: A note showing urgent financial pressure.
evidence.discoveryConditions: Search service area or ask about money request.

evidence.title: Private envelope
evidence.description: A red-herring document unrelated to the watch.

scenes.openingNarration: The investigation begins in the study with three suspects present and the watch missing from an apparently locked case.
likelyPlayerQuestions: When was the watch last seen?

safeAnswers.Who was present?: The three NPCs in the room.

hiddenFactsNotYetRevealed: The rear panel was prepared earlier.

scenes.openingNarration: The front lock is clean; the rear of the case is more interesting.
likelyPlayerQuestions: Can the case open another way?
hiddenFactsNotYetRevealed: The watch left through the rear panel.

scenes.openingNarration: The clock is not behaving as it should.
hiddenFactsNotYetRevealed: The missing object is inside until discovered.

scenes.openingNarration: Each person has a different reason to be uneasy.

safeAnswers.Who had access?: The owner had front-key access; Thomas had preparation access.

hiddenFactsNotYetRevealed: The motive clue should be discoverable, not volunteered too early.

scenes.entryConditions: Player has enough proof or finds the hidden object.

discoveryRules.triggerDescription: Inspect rear panel of display case.
caseBoardUpdates: The front lock may not be the entry point.

discoveryRules.triggerDescription: Inspect, listen to, or open the clock.
caseBoardUpdates: The clock is part of the evidence chain.

discoveryRules.triggerDescription: Search service area or ask about recent money request.
caseBoardUpdates: A concealed money pressure may explain motive.

discoveryRules.triggerDescription: Ask Celia about the private envelope.
caseBoardUpdates: One apparent money clue has an innocent explanation.

discoveryRules.triggerDescription: Ask Thomas about movements without first raising the clock.
caseBoardUpdates: Thomas may know too much about an object not yet discussed.

discoveryRules.triggerDescription: Accuse with method, motive, opportunity, and proof.

knownFacts: The watch was last seen in a locked case.

assetManifest.title: Study overview
assetManifest.textFallback: The study contains desk, fireplace, display case, clock, tea trolley, lamp, and window.

assetManifest.title: Display case
assetManifest.textFallback: The case has an intact front lock and disturbed rear panel.

assetManifest.title: Tallcase clock
assetManifest.textFallback: The clock has stopped and its lower compartment shows recent disturbance.

assetManifest.title: Pocket watch
assetManifest.textFallback: The missing object is found where the evidence points.

knownRiskAreas: Verify motive is discoverable before final reveal.
Verify hiding place is not revealed too early.
